meaning: adj.不加批判的,无批判力的,不严厉的
examples: Disciplined, uncritical loyalty to political power became his compass for the choices he had to make.
Her uncritical acceptance of everything I said began to irritate me.
"AI is hugely exciting," she said, "but it has limitations, which present grave dangers given uncritical use."
It is not as though Mr. Feith has been uncritical of the Bush administration.
An uncritical reading of Enron's inscrutable reports indicates it was running so much faster than everyone else.
